channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-20109] DKMS RPM spec file declares that it provides client binaries
item.description:
Hi,

We manage the Lustre kernel modules on our clients using the lustre-client-dkms rpm package and have noticed that it incorrectly declares it "provides" lustre-client, when it does not.

If lustre-client-dkms is installed before lustre-client, idempotent configuration management tools like puppet can believe that lustre-client has already been installed as it has already been "provided".

Seems to be a problem in 2.12.x, 2.15.x, 2.17.x, etc.

e.g.

# create a basic yum repository
cd /root/packages
wget https://downloads.whamcloud.com/public/lustre/latest-release/el8.10/client/RPMS/x86_64/lustre-client-2.15.8-1.el8.x86_64.rpm
wget https://downloads.whamcloud.com/public/lustre/latest-release/el8.10/client/RPMS/x86_64/lustre-client-dkms-2.15.8-1.el8.noarch.rpm
wget https://yum.puppetlabs.com/puppet7/el/8/x86_64/puppet-agent-7.9.0-1.el8.x86_64.rpm
createrepo .

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/packages.repo <<EOF
[packages]
name = local packages
baseurl = file:///root/packages
enabled = 1
gpgcheck = 0
EOF

# install puppet
dnf install puppet-agent

# ATTEMPT 1
# ask puppet to install lustre-client-dkms and then lustre-client

[root@test ~]# puppet resource package lustre-client-dkms ensure=installed
Notice: /Package[lustre-client-dkms]/ensure: created
package { 'lustre-client-dkms':
  ensure   => '2.15.8-1.el8',
  provider => 'dnf',
}

[root@test ~]# rpm -q -a | grep lustre
lustre-client-dkms-2.15.8-1.el8.noarch

[root@test ~]# puppet resource package lustre-client ensure=installed
package { 'lustre-client':
  ensure   => '2.15.8-1.el8',
  provider => 'dnf',
}

# ATTEMPT 1 RESULT: only lustre-client-dkms installed
[root@test ~]# rpm -q -a | grep lustre
lustre-client-dkms-2.15.8-1.el8.noarch

# reset...
[root@test ~]# rpm -e lustre-client-dkms lustre-client
[root@test ~]# rpm -q -a | grep lustre


# ATTEMPT 2
# ask puppet to install lustre-client and then lustre-client-dkms

[root@slurm ~]# puppet resource package lustre-client ensure=installed
Notice: /Package[lustre-client]/ensure: created
package { 'lustre-client':
  ensure   => '2.15.8-1.el8',
  provider => 'dnf',
}

[root@slurm ~]# rpm -q -a | grep lustre
lustre-client-2.15.8-1.el8.x86_64
lustre-client-dkms-2.15.8-1.el8.noarch

[root@slurm ~]# puppet resource package lustre-client-dkms ensure=installed
package { 'lustre-client-dkms':
  ensure   => '2.15.8-1.el8',
  provider => 'dnf',
}

# ATTEMPT 2 RESULT: both lustre-client and lustre-client-dkms installed
[root@slurm ~]# rpm -q -a | grep lustre
lustre-client-2.15.8-1.el8.x86_64
lustre-client-dkms-2.15.8-1.el8.noarch

 

Problem appears to stem from lustre-dkms.spec.in containing:

%if %{with servers}
%if %{with zfs}
Provides:       %{lustre_name}-osd-zfs = %{version}
Provides:       kmod-%{lustre_name}-osd-zfs = %{version}
Provides:       %{lustre_name}-osd-zfs-mount = %{version}
%endif
%if %{with ldiskfs}
Provides:       %{lustre_name}-osd-ldiskfs = %{version}
Provides:       kmod-%{lustre_name}-osd-ldiskfs = %{version}
Provides:       %{lustre_name}-osd-ldiskfs-mount = %{version}
%endif
Provides:       %{lustre_name}-osd
%else
Provides:       %{lustre_name}-client
%endif

Ignoring the "with servers" case (because I've never used it), the "Provides: %{lustre_name}client" line probably needs to be changed to "Provides: kmod%{lustre_name}-client" - seems to do the trick for us.
item.environment: First noticed on Rocky 8 / ppc64le / Lustre 2.12, but is a general issue.
item.summary: DKMS RPM spec file declares that it provides client binaries

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
You're welcome, bodgerer.
Yes, kmod-lustre-client is provided by:

%define lustre_name @PACKAGE@-client
# ......
%global kmod_name kmod-%{lustre_name}
# ......
Provides:       %{kmod_name} = %{version}

Thanks Jian Yu

Seems to have done the trick - the dkms package no longer provides lustre-client but does still provide kmod-lustre-client (as it should).

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/65210
Subject: LU-20109 build: remove lustre-client from lustre-dkms.spec.in
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 9499cb061eff5aa5f18180c3e754753c1cc710e2

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/65208
Subject: LU-20109 build: remove lustre-client from lustre-dkms.spec.in
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: d66e0a620586310b1aa9f97b0b739963cdc2707f

For Lustre 2.15.8:

# rpm -q --provides lustre-client-dkms-2.15.8-1.el8.noarch.rpm 
kmod-lustre-client = 2.15.8
lustre-client
lustre-client-dkms = 2.15.8-1.el8

The same issue also exists on master branch:

# rpm -q --provides lustre-client-dkms-2.17.51_73_g472e0f2-1.el8.noarch.rpm 
config(lustre-client-dkms) = 2.17.51_73_g472e0f2-1.el8
kmod-lustre-client = 2.17.51_73_g472e0f2
lustre-client
lustre-client-dkms = 2.17.51_73_g472e0f2-1.el8
lustre-modules = 2.17.51_73_g472e0f2

Working on a patch.
Let me take a look at this issue.

hbennett  we need help to assign this to a Wham Cloud engineer.  

CC; ipoddubnyy

Apologies, that quote from lustre-dkms.spec.in was actually from lustre-dkms.spec.

The suggested change to lustre-dkms.spec.in should be from:

Provides: @PACKAGE@-client

to:

Provides: kmod-@PACKAGE@-client

Also, we have an active support contract (I should have put "(Durham University)" in the subject line).
